title: A desk and a rented lamp
note: June starts freelancing out of a Copenhagen apartment — logos, posters, and websites for anyone who would say yes. The habit of shipping starts here.

title: The first real product
note: A challenger bank hires us to design onboarding. Sixty thousand people walk through it in a year, and we stop thinking of design as decoration.

title: Amber becomes a studio
note: Two more designers join. We write down the way we already worked — discovery first, no hand-offs, ship in weeks — and put a name on the door.

title: Design meets the codebase
note: A design engineer joins and the Figma-to-production gap closes. Clients start getting components instead of screenshots.

title: Four people, sixty-one launches
note: Deliberately still four. We take two studio slots a quarter, which is the only way the work stays this close to the people making it.
